subject: Re: Abivax ERP - NetSuite Biotech Expert Implementors  Introduction
bodyText:
Hi Micheal,

I'm at AC (starts at 3:30PM) so if you don't hear form me for a few hours - and also forvige any quick half baked thoughts, but I didn't want to keep you hanging in the meantime.

We have a couple options for PMO, I think regardless of how the Company moves forward, we can have our IT SOX Manager (ex EY Paris) who is French French support any extent of PMO.  I like this for two reasons.


1.	
	She's French - Parisian.  She also lives in the US and is under my umbrella/on my SOX team.  This means that while she can work in both countries, she can also support the Company from both a language and two location perspective.  Obviously, she's IT SOX so having her closely involved will mean another advantage from an SDLC perspective
2.	
	While she's not an implementor, I don't see why she couldn't be the PMO and be supervised or paired up with PMO from our implementation team.  


Whether our team implements NetSuite or is the extension of your team from a PMO perspective (e.g., to ensure process owners know what the Netsuite Services Partners are asking them - often these fail because the business doesn't understand what the implementors are asking in practical terms), we are really flexible/open to what your needs are. 

Selfishly, I like the having the embedded IT SOX Manager regardless, so we can keep her focused in being that intermediary and your right hand.  She's got French language and IT SOX and since she's in my practice tree - we have a bit more supervision than just some random PMO person.

I'd also expect her to ongoing work with Walid and/or Youness who are very close to all the processes.  I think there are a lot of options here - the devil, as always, in the details.  Knowing the organization, I want to make sure there are people I know and trust who will take good care of Trinidad + Paris team, while also being able to support all of Hema & US team's needs. 

Open to all thoughts. 

This is always more fun/better over coffee!

subject: RE: Concur Access for Reimbursements
bodyText:
Hi Kimberly – I haven’t received a welcome email from concur.

 

From: Kimberly Gordon <kimberly.gordon-ext@abivax.com> 
Sent: Monday, March 2, 2026 2:11 PM
To: Michael Markman <mike.markman@abivax.com>
Cc: Matt Epley <Matt.Epley@abivax.com>
Subject: RE: Concur Access for Reimbursements 

 

Hi Mike,

 

I wanted to let you know that we have set up your Concur profile and you should have received a welcome email for it. 

 

It might allow you to set up your own password without logging in, but if it asks for a password, we used Abivax3226! for a temporary password. If you log in with this temporary password, please change it to a new one once you are in the system. 

 

As a brief overview of how our Concur process works, reports will go to your immediate manager and then to us here in finance. Once both of those reviews are completed, it goes into an “Expense Pay” batch. These batches close on the 10th and 25th of each month, with the payments ending up in employee’s accounts directly from Concur within a couple of business days after each batch closes. 

 

To submit a report, you must input your personal banking information where you’d like to receive the reimbursement, which can be found in your profile settings. 

 

As always, do not hesitate to reach out with any questions as you begin to use the system. 

 

Best regards,

Kimberly Gordon
Staff Accountant

subject: PTP Process + your trip in Paris
bodyText:
Hello Mike, I hope you are well?

 

It seems you have a few questions for me about PTP processes, and you'll probably want to set up a meeting when you're in Paris the week of March 16.

First, can I provide you with some documentation or answers to your most urgent questions, and second, do you already have a rough idea of how your days in Paris will be organized? I think that if we are going to meet in person, it would be a good idea to schedule an appointment so that I can organize my teleworking days (for your information, I telework on Mondays and Thursdays).

 

In any case, I'm available if you have any questions, either by email or Teams.

My spoken English is a little rusty, but I'm sure we'll manage to understand each other. 

Have a great day!
